original: Ezra reads the Book of the Law to the Israelites in Jerusalem (see Nehemiah 8:1, NLTse)
Empress Irene of Byzantium driven out
Forces of Holy Roman Emperor Charles V begin a siege of the French-held city of Metz; siege eventually fails and is called off in January 1553
Leiden University Library opens its doors after its founding in 1575
English, French & Dutch delegates sign anti-Spanish "Triple Covenant"
Laurens Reael resigns as governor-general of Dutch East Indies
Earthquake in Safed, Palestine kills hundreds
Execution of Girondins in Paris during Reign of Terror
Holland Brigade battle at Durango, Spain
Cornishman Sir Humphry Davy patents miner's safety lamp
Edinburgh-based body snatchers William Burke and William Hare are exposed for murdering 16 people and selling the corpses to medical schools
Collision of river boat Monmouth & Trement on Mississippi; 300 die
Donner party, unable to cross the Donner Pass, construct a winter camp
The Maori Wars resumed as British forces in New Zealand led by General Duncan Cameron began their Invasion of the Waikato
Nevada admitted as 36th state of the Union
Australia's first royal tour begins as Prince Albert, son of Queen Victoria arrives in Adelaide, South Australia
Standard uniform approved for US postal carriers
Founding of Netherland Protestant Union in Dokkum
Great Backerganj Cyclone of 1876 ravages British India (modern-day Bangladesh), killing over 200,000
Metropolitan club plays its last game of its non-league season. They win 80 of 151 games (18-43 versus NL teams)
Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov's orchestral work "Capriccio Espagnol" premieres in St Petersburg, Russia
Scottish vet John Boyd Dunlop patents pneumatic bicycle tyre
Exposition Universelle (World's Fair) in Paris closes after 32 million visitors
Great revolutionary demonstration for amnesty in St Petersburg
Calgary City Rugby Football Club's 1st game defeating Strathcona Rugby Foot-ball Club 15-0 at Calgary
IV Summer Olympic Games close at White City Stadium in London, England
1st US paved coast-to-coast highway, the Lincoln Highway is dedicated
Great Britain & France declare war on Turkey
Clare Kummer's play "Good Gracious Annabelle" premieres in NYC
World War I: Battle of Beersheba in southern Palestine - "last successful cavalry charge in history" is performed by the 4th Australian Light Horse
Short-lived Banat Republic founded in territory where Romania, Hungary and Serbia meet
Romania annexes Bessarabia
Federation Sportive Feminine International forms (1st woman track & field association)
Karel & Josef Čapek's play "World We Live In" premieres in NYC
160 consecutive days of 100 degrees F begin at Marble Bar, Australia
World Savings Day announced in Milan, Italy, by the Members of the Association at the 1st International Savings Bank Congress (World Society of Savings Banks)
Cossack officer Reza Chan replaces sultan Ahmad as Shah of Persia
Failed assassination attempt onBenito Mussoliniby 15-year-old Anteo Zamboni, who was lynched on the spot
The Boy Scouts of the Philippines formed
Spanish government moves from Valencia to Barcelona
Great Depression: In an effort to try restore investor confidence, the New York Stock Exchange unveils a fifteen-point program aimed to upgrade protection for the investing public.
Clothing factory fire in Huddersfield, England kills 49
Mount Rushmore Monument is completed in South Dakota, designed by Gutzon Borglum
Prior to US joining WWII, German submarine U-552 torpedoes US destroyer Reuben James near Iceland
9th day of the Battle of El Alamein
U-boats sink and damage 120 allied ships this month (659,457 tons)
World War II: F4U Corsair accomplishes the first successful radar-guided interception.
Chief of staff Kruls names De Quay chairman of Universal Commission
Dutch Nazi Henri 'Hakkie' Holdert, director of Amsterdam paper De Telegraaf and member of the SS, sentenced to 12 years imprisonment
WOC (now KWQC) TV channel 6 in Davenport, IA (NBC) 1st broadcast
French Second Chamber accepts the Schuman Plan
1st thermonuclear bomb detonated at Marshall Islands
TV broadcasting begins in Belgium
Algerian Revolution against French begins
KREM TV channel 2 in Spokane, WA (CBS/ABC) begins broadcasting
Bernardus Johannes Alfrink installed as Archbishop of Utrecht
American Navy pilot Conrad 'Gus' Shinn is first person to land a plane at the South Pole
Britain and France join Israeli forces in Egypt and begin to bomb Egypt to reopen Suez Canal
Brooklyn, NY ends streetcar service
Rear Adm GJ Dufek becomes 1st American to step foot on the South Pole
USSR and Egypt sign contracts to build the Aswan High Dam
Cyclone hits coast of Gulf of Bengal; about 10,000 die
Federal judge rules that laws in Birmingham, Alabama, against integrated playing fields are illegal
Hurricane Hattie kills 400 in British Honduras
Leaking propane gas explodes kills 64 at 'Holiday on Ice' in Indiana
KIMO TV channel 13 in Anchorage, AK (ABC) begins broadcasting
SF Giants Mike McCormick wins NL Cy Young Award
Milwaukee Bucks win their 1st game beating Detroit 138-118 (6th game)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Eastside race riot in Jacksonville, Florida
The Irish Republican Army (IRA) explode a bomb at the Post Office Tower in London
2 Catholic children (6 and 4) playing on the street are killed in a Ulster Freedom Fighters (UFF) car bomb attack on a bar in Ship Street, Belfast
Three Provisional Irish Republican Army volunteers escape from Mountjoy Prison in Dublin using a hijacked helicopter
Dutch Marines end hostage crisis in Scheveningen prison
Iranian oil workers go on strike
People's Democratic Republic of Yemen (South Yemen) adopts constitution
Baltimore Orioles pitcher Mike Flanagan wins AL Cy Young Award
US DC-10 crashes at Mexico-City, 74 killed
Julian Nott sets world hot-air balloon altitude record (16,806 m)
Polish government recognizes Solidarity trade union
Senegal routes troops to Gambia due to Libyan threat
1st live US radio drama in 25 years (Halloween Story on NBC)
Ron Grant completes a 217 day, 8,316 mile run around Australia
Howard Goodall & Melvyn Bragg's musical "Hired Man" premieres in London
Puerto Rican tanker 'San Francisco' explodes spilling 2 million gallons of oil as ship caught fire
New Zealand author Keri Hulme is the first, first-time novelist to win the Booker Prize for "The Bone People"
A pair in Coventry, England, ties world record for longest singles tennis match at 80 hrs 21 minutes
American jockeyChris Antleybecomes the first rider to win nine races in a day (four at Aqueduct and five at The Meadowlands)
19°F lowest October temperature ever recorded in Cleveland, Ohio
First Monday Night NFL game played in Indianapolis; Colts beat Denver Broncos, 55-23
Journalists demand greater press freedom in Yugoslavia
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Turgat Ozal elected president of Turkey
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Pakistan make 3-0 drubbing of NZ, Waqar Younis 29 series wkts
Palestinians attend US mideast peace talks in Madrid
Don Keller makes his 18,000th sky dive
25 people killed during Ghana-Ivory Coast soccer match
German unemployment hits national record of 3.5 million
"Creep" single released by TLC – their 1st US No.1 (Billboard Song of the Year 1995)
American Eagle ATR-72 crash down at Gary, Indiana: 68 killed
American jam band Phish performs The Beatles "White Album" as a musical costume during a show at the Glens Falls Civic Center in Glens Falls, New York.
American jam band Phish performs The Who's "Quadrophenia" as a musical costume during a show at the Rosemont Horizon in Rosemont, Illinois
NHL NJ Devils agree to stay in NJ
American jam band Phish performs The Talking Heads' "Remain In Light" as a musical costume during a show at the Omni Coliseum in Atlanta, Georgia
Transportes Aéreos Regionais (TAM) Flight 402, a Fokker F100, crashes into several houses in São Paulo, Brazil killing 98 including 2 on the ground.
American jam band Phish performs The Velvet Underground's "Rock and Roll" as a musical costume during a show at the Thomas & Mack Center at the University of Nevada in Paradise, Nevada
Iraq disarmament crisis begins: Iraq announces it would no longer cooperate with United Nations weapons inspectors.
EgyptAir Flight 990 traveling from New York City to Cairo crashes off the coast of Nantucket, Massachusetts, killing all 217 on-board.
Finnish McLaren driver Mika Häkkinen wins season ending Japanese Grand Prix at Suzuka to clinch his second straight F1 World Drivers Championship; wins by 2 points from Irishman Eddie Irvine
Roman Catholic Church and Lutheran Church leaders sign the Joint Declaration on the Doctrine of Justification, ending a centuries-old doctrinal dispute over the nature of faith and salvation.
Yachtsman Jesse Martin returns to Melbourne after 11 months of circumnavigating the world, solo, non-stop and unassisted.
A chartered Antonov An-26 explodes after takeoff in Northern Angola killing 50
A Singapore Airlines Boeing 747-400 operating as Flight 006 collides with construction equipment upon takeoff in Taipei, Taiwan killing 79 passengers and four crew members
PopeJohn Paul IIdeclares Saint Thomas More as "the heavenly Patron of Statesmen and Politicians"
The last Multics (Multiplexed Information and Computing Service) machine shut down

A federal grand jury in Houston, Texas formally indicted former Enron Corp. chief financial officer Andrew Fastow on 78 counts of wire fraud, money laundering, conspiracy and obstruction of justice related to the collapse of his ex-employer.
A bankruptcy court approves MCI's reorganization plans, essentially clearing the telecommunications company to exit bankruptcy

Whitepaper "Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System" is published by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto. It describes a decentralized peer-to-peer network that could track and verify transactions while producing a transparent, verifiable record.
Belgium leaves the recession with 0.5% growth in the third quarter
The world population reaches 7 billion inhabitants according to the United Nations
The New York Stock Exchange opens after being closed for two days after Hurricane Sandy
Russian airliner crashes killing all 224 on board in Sinai Peninsula, Egypt - Russia's worst air disaster
Engineers at MIT announce they have engineered spinach to send emails when detecting explosive materials in groundwater as part of plant nanobionic research
Lebanon parliament elects Michel Aoun President after 2 1/2 years without a leader
John Kelly, White House Chief of Staff comments in TV interview that “the lack of an ability to compromise led to the Civil War", draws criticism
Judge on Maui, Hawaii orders man to write ex-girlfriend 144 compliments after violating protection order to send 144 nasty text messages
Takahiro Shiraishi arrested in Zama, Japan, a day after nine dismembered bodies found in his apartment
Terrorist attack in New York when a truck mows down people on a cycle lane, killing 8, injuring 10
Two men convicted of raping and impregnating their 10-year-old niece in Chandigarh, India
In landmark verdict, Pakistani Supreme Court acquits Christian woman of blasphemy against the prophet Mohammed after eight years on death row
US and Great Britain call for a cease-fire in Saudi-led war in Yemen, in 3-year war that has claimed over 10,000 lives and created famine conditions
Fire destroys most of historic Shuri Castle, once seat of the Ryukyu Kingdom, on island of Okinawa, Japan
Gas canister explodes on a train in Rahim Yar Khan, Pakaistan killing at least 70 and injuring 30
Jihadist group Islamic State names Abu Ibrahim al-Hashemi al-Qurashi as its new leader after US special forces kill its former leader
New Zealand Rugby Union team routs Australia, 43-5 in Sydney to retain Bledisloe Cup (2-1) for 18th straight series; Wallabies' largest ever defeat by All Blacks
World's largest solar farm goes live in Sirindhorn reservoir, Thailand, as a hydro-floating solar hybrid system the size of 70 football fields

American rapper Young Thug (33) pleads guilty to street gang racketeering charges and no contest to related weapons and drug charges, ending a prolonged trial in Atlanta, Georgia; judge sentences him to time served plus 15 years of probation
Russian legal claims against Google in Russia reaches two undecillion roubles ($20 decillion), largely symbolic for blocking Russian channels on YouTube
Michelangelofinishes painting "The Last Judgment" on the altar wall of the Sistine Chape in Vatican City
Parliamentary cryptographerJohn Wallisgives his Inaugural lecture as newly appointed Savilian Professor of Geometry at Oxford University,
George Frideric Handel's opera "Tamerlano" premieres at the King's Theatre in London
Giacomo Casanovaescapes from "The Leads" prison in Venice by climbing onto the roof
John Dalton's first lecture to the Manchester Literary/Philosophical Society, "Extraordinary facts relating to the vision of colours"

Deadline for Warsaw Jews to move into the Warsaw Ghetto

J. Edgar Hoover's last meeting with PresidentJohn F. Kennedy
Barbra Streisand's "People" album goes #1 for five weeks
Nguyễn Văn Thiệutakes oath of office to become the 1st President of the Second Republic of South Vietnam
Linda Eastmanmoves to UK permanently
US PresidentLyndon B. Johnsonorders a halt to all bombing of North Vietnam
George Harrison's "Something" is released by the Beatles as a single in UK, his first "A" side
Jim Morrisonis sentenced to six months in jail and a $500 fine for indecent exposure and open profanity, though remains free on a $50,000 bond pending appeal
Cleveland Indians pitcherGaylord Perrywins AL Cy Young award

Irish singer-songwriterBob Geldof's first appearance with The Boomtown Rats

RapperTupac Shakurcharged with aggravated assault

American tennis starVenus Williamsmakes her professional debut as a 14-year-old with a 6-3, 6-4 win over former NCAA champion and world No. 58 Shaun Stafford in the Bank of the West Classic in Oakland, California
British au pairLouise Woodward, 19, sentenced to life for the death of Matthew Eappen 8½ months (judge changes to time served)
Bethany Hamilton, aged 13, has her arm bitten off by a shark while surfing in Hawaii
